name: Apple II / II+ / IIe
description: Standard Apple ][ family symbol table: Monitor ROM entry points, soft switches, zero-page conventions, screen-hole bytes, slot I/O page. Targets II/II+ Autostart Monitor and IIe Enhanced Monitor where they agree; IIe-only entries are marked.

sources.name: Sather, Jim - Understanding the Apple IIe (Quality Software, 1985)
Apple II Monitor ROM disassembly (Apple Computer, 1978)

0x0026.name: GBASL
0x0026.comment: Graphics base address low (HGR/GR plot routines)

0x0028.name: BASL
0x0028.comment: Text base address low (line of current cursor row)

0x002A.name: BAS2L
0x002A.comment: Secondary text base address low (used by SCROLL)

0x002C.name: H2
0x002C.comment: End column for horizontal line / right edge for HLINE

0x002D.name: V2
0x002D.comment: End row for vertical line / bottom for VLINE

0x002E.name: MASK
0x002E.comment: HGR plot mask / hires color mask

0x0030.name: COLOR
0x0030.comment: Lo-res color byte (color in both nibbles)

0x0032.name: INVFLG
0x0032.comment: Inverse/flash flag for COUT (mask AND'd into char: $FF=normal, $7F=flash, $3F=inverse)

0x0033.name: PROMPT
0x0033.comment: Monitor prompt character (default ']' from Applesoft, '*' for monitor, '>' for Integer)

0x0036.name: CSWL
0x0036.comment: Character output hook low (COUT JMPs through (CSW)); $FDF0 = COUT1 (default screen output)

0x0038.name: KSWL
0x0038.comment: Keyboard input hook low (RDKEY JMPs through (KSW)); $FD1B = KEYIN (default keyboard input)

0x0045_dup.name: ACC
0x0045_dup.comment: Saved A register (Monitor; alias of A5H)

0x004E.name: RNDL
0x004E.comment: Random number low (incremented by RDKEY while waiting)

screen_holes.comment: Bytes inside the unused regions of the text/lo-res page that the firmware uses as per-slot scratch. Each slot N (1..7) gets one byte at +$78+N, +$7B+N, +$F8+N, +$FB+N within pages $04, $05, $06, $07. Slot-N firmware is at $Cn00 (Cn=$C1..$C7) and uses these slots' screen holes for state.

0x0478.name: SCRN_HOLE_0478_S0
0x0478.comment: Screen-hole base; slot N adds N (e.g. slot 3 -> $047B). Per-card use varies; Videx firmware uses $0478+N as CRFLAG

0x05F8.name: SCRN_HOLE_05F8_S0
0x05F8.comment: Screen-hole; slot N -> $05F8+N. Pascal protocol byte 5 (status bits) per Tech Note Misc #8 lives here for slot N

0x0778.name: SCRN_HOLE_0778_S0
0x0778.comment: Screen-hole; slot N -> $0778+N. Videx uses $0778+N as POFF (power-on flag)

0x07F8.name: SCRN_HOLE_07F8_S0
0x07F8.comment: Screen-hole; slot N -> $07F8+N. By convention this byte holds $Cn (the high byte of the active slot's $Cn00) when that slot's firmware owns CSW or KSW

0x03F2.name: SOFTEV
0x03F2.comment: Reset (warm-start) vector; chained through $03F4 power-up byte

0x03F4.name: PWREDUP
0x03F4.comment: Power-up byte: must equal SOFTEV-high XOR $A5; if not, autostart performs cold boot

0x03FE.name: IRQV
0x03FE.comment: IRQ vector (Monitor JMPs through ($03FE) for non-BRK IRQs)

soft_switches.comment: I/O page $C000-$C0FF. Reading vs writing these addresses can have different effects; many are 'either read or write' to toggle. IIe adds switches above $C050.

0xC000.name: KBD
0xC000.comment: Read: keyboard data (high bit set when key pending). IIe write: CLR80STORE

0xC001.name: SET80STORE
0xC001.comment: IIe write: enable 80STORE (PAGE2 controls aux RAM main page mapping)

0xC008.name: SETSTDZP
0xC008.comment: IIe write: use main zero page + stack + LC

0xC009.name: SETALTZP
0xC009.comment: IIe write: use aux zero page + stack + LC

0xC00A.name: SETINTC3ROM
0xC00A.comment: IIe write: slot 3 ROM = internal (80-col firmware)

0xC00B.name: SETSLOTC3ROM
0xC00B.comment: IIe write: slot 3 ROM = peripheral card (e.g. Videx)

0xC00E.name: CLRALTCHAR
0xC00E.comment: IIe write: primary character set (uppercase + flashing)

0xC00F.name: SETALTCHAR
0xC00F.comment: IIe write: alternate character set (MouseText, no flashing)

0xC010.name: KBDSTRB
0xC010.comment: Read or write: clear keyboard strobe (acknowledge keypress). IIe read: also returns AKD (any-key-down) in bit 7

0xC011.name: RDLCBNK2
0xC011.comment: IIe read bit 7: language card bank 2 selected

0xC012.name: RDLCRAM
0xC012.comment: IIe read bit 7: language card RAM read selected

0xC013.name: RDRAMRD
0xC013.comment: IIe read bit 7: aux RAM is currently being read

0xC014.name: RDRAMWRT
0xC014.comment: IIe read bit 7: aux RAM is currently being written

0xC015.name: RDCXROM
0xC015.comment: IIe read bit 7: $C100-$CFFF is internal ROM (not slot ROM)

0xC016.name: RDALTZP
0xC016.comment: IIe read bit 7: aux zero page is currently selected

0xC017.name: RDC3ROM
0xC017.comment: IIe read bit 7: slot 3 ROM = peripheral (not internal 80-col)

0xC019.name: RDVBL
0xC019.comment: IIe read bit 7: vertical blanking active

0xC01A.name: RDTEXT
0xC01A.comment: IIe read bit 7: TEXT mode is set

0xC01B.name: RDMIXED
0xC01B.comment: IIe read bit 7: MIXED mode is set

0xC01C.name: RDPAGE2
0xC01C.comment: IIe read bit 7: PAGE2 is selected

0xC01D.name: RDHIRES
0xC01D.comment: IIe read bit 7: HIRES mode is set

0xC01E.name: RDALTCHAR
0xC01E.comment: IIe read bit 7: alternate character set is selected

0xC01F.name: RD80VID
0xC01F.comment: IIe read bit 7: 80-column video is on

0xC055.name: TXTPAGE2
0xC055.comment: Display PAGE 2 ($800/$4000) -- or with 80STORE on IIe, select aux memory mapping. Also: HISCR

0xC058.name: SETAN0
0xC058.comment: Set annunciator 0 output low (II/II+); IIe: same. Used by Videx 80-col card to signal 'turn on 80 columns'

0xC059.name: CLRAN0
0xC059.comment: Set annunciator 0 output high (II/II+); IIe: same. Videx firmware writes here to enable 80-col mode

0xC05C.name: SETAN2
0xC05C.comment: Annunciator 2 low. IIe: also DHIRES off when IOUDIS

0xC05D.name: CLRAN2
0xC05D.comment: Annunciator 2 high. IIe: also DHIRES on when IOUDIS

0xC05E.name: SETAN3
0xC05E.comment: Annunciator 3 low. IIe: enable double hi-res (when IOUDIS off)

0xC05F.name: CLRAN3
0xC05F.comment: Annunciator 3 high. IIe: disable double hi-res

0xC060.name: TAPEIN
0xC060.comment: Cassette input (read bit 7). IIe: also BUTN3 (Shift key on II+ via mod)

0xC070.name: PTRIG
0xC070.comment: Trigger paddle timers (start one-shots, ~3ms typical full-scale)

0xC07E.name: SETIOUDIS
0xC07E.comment: IIe write: disable IOUDIS (enables DHIRES + AN2/AN3 reuse)

0xC07F.name: CLRIOUDIS
0xC07F.comment: IIe write: enable IOUDIS (re-enables AN2/AN3 as plain annunciators)

0xC080.name: LC_RAM_BANK2_RD
0xC080.comment: Language card: read RAM bank 2 ($D000-$DFFF), write protect

0xC081.name: LC_ROM_BANK2_WR
0xC081.comment: Language card: read ROM, write enable bank 2 (read twice to enable write)

0xC082.name: LC_ROM_RD
0xC082.comment: Language card: read ROM, write protect

0xC083.name: LC_RAM_BANK2_RDWR
0xC083.comment: Language card: read/write RAM bank 2 (read twice to enable write)

0xC088.name: LC_RAM_BANK1_RD
0xC088.comment: Language card: read RAM bank 1, write protect

0xC089.name: LC_ROM_BANK1_WR
0xC089.comment: Language card: read ROM, write enable bank 1

0xC08A.name: LC_ROM_RD2
0xC08A.comment: Language card: read ROM, write protect (alias)

slot_io.comment: Each slot N (1..7) is decoded at $C0N0-$C0NF (16 bytes of device select). The slot's firmware ROM is at $CN00-$CNFF. Slot 3 firmware is overlaid by IIe internal 80-col firmware unless SETSLOTC3ROM ($C00B) is asserted.

0xC0E0.name: SLOT6_DEVSEL
0xC0E0.comment: Slot 6 device select page (Disk II: see disk_ii_softswitches)

0xC300.name: SLOT3_ROM
0xC300.comment: Slot 3 firmware ROM (or IIe internal 80-col firmware)

0xC600.name: SLOT6_ROM
0xC600.comment: Slot 6 firmware ROM (Disk II boot ROM, entry $C600 = boot)

0xC800.name: EXP_ROM
0xC800.comment: Shared expansion ROM window ($C800-$CFFE); a slot 'wins' it by accessing $CN00..$CNFF and releases it by accessing $CFFF

0xCFFF.name: EXPROMOFF
0xCFFF.comment: Read or write: release expansion ROM (no slot owns $C800-$CFFE until next $CN00 access)

disk_ii_softswitches.comment: Slot N Disk II controller. Slot 6 is canonical. Replace base $C0E0 with $C0(N+8)0 for other slots.

0xC0EC.name: DISKII_Q6L
0xC0EC.comment: Q6L: read latch (or read mode of LSS)

0xC0ED.name: DISKII_Q6H
0xC0ED.comment: Q6H: load latch from data bus (write data byte to disk)

0xC0EE.name: DISKII_Q7L
0xC0EE.comment: Q7L: set LSS to READ mode (also clears WP latch read)

0xC0EF.name: DISKII_Q7H
0xC0EF.comment: Q7H: set LSS to WRITE mode

monitor_rom.comment: Apple II Autostart Monitor entry points (II/II+, ROM at $F800-$FFFF). Most addresses unchanged on IIe Enhanced; IIe-specific entries noted.

0xF80E.name: PLOT1
0xF80E.comment: Lo-res plot helper, BASL/BASH already set

0xF847.name: GBASCALC
0xF847.comment: Compute lo-res base address into (GBASL,GBASH); A=row

0xFB2F.name: TEXT
0xFB2F.comment: Set text mode + reset window to full screen

0xFB5B.name: BASCALC
0xFB5B.comment: Compute text-line base address into (BASL,BASH); A=row

0xFB78.name: VIDOUT
0xFB78.comment: Print A on screen at cursor (no scroll, no control-char interpretation)

0xFBFD.name: VIDWAIT
0xFBFD.comment: Output A via VIDOUT, then wait if needed

0xFC24.name: VTABZ_DUP
0xFC24.comment: VTAB inner entry (same as $FB4B on II+)

0xFC42.name: CLREOP
0xFC42.comment: Clear from cursor to end of page

0xFC66.name: LF
0xFC66.comment: Line feed (advance row, scroll if needed)

0xFC70.name: SCROLL
0xFC70.comment: Scroll text window up one line

0xFC9C.name: CLREOL
0xFC9C.comment: Clear from cursor to end of line

0xFCB4.name: NXTA4
0xFCB4.comment: Increment A4, compare to A2 (MOVE/VERIFY loop)

0xFD0C.name: RDKEY
0xFD0C.comment: Read keystroke via (KSW). Default routes to KEYIN. Returns char in A (high bit set)

0xFD18.name: KEYIN_ENTRY
0xFD18.comment: RDKEY's default keyboard entry (loaded into KSW at boot)

0xFD1B.name: KEYIN
0xFD1B.comment: Real keyboard input: spin until KBD bit 7 set; clear strobe; return char in A

0xFD35.name: RDCHAR
0xFD35.comment: Read character with ESC handling for cursor moves

0xFD6A.name: GETLN
0xFD6A.comment: Read line into IN ($0200), prompt = (PROMPT). Returns X = length

0xFDE3.name: PRHEX
0xFDE3.comment: Print A low nibble as 1 hex digit

0xFDED.name: COUT
0xFDED.comment: Output character A via (CSW). Default routes to COUT1 (screen)

0xFDF0.name: COUT1
0xFDF0.comment: Screen-only COUT entry (loaded into CSW at boot)

0xFE1F.name: IDROUTINE
0xFE1F.comment: IIe: identification routine - returns $06 in A for IIe family

0xFE89.name: SETKBD
0xFE89.comment: Reset KSW to default keyboard input ($FD1B); CSWL,KSWL := $1B,$FD

0xFE93.name: SETVID
0xFE93.comment: Reset CSW to default screen output ($FDF0); CSWL,CSWH := $F0,$FD

0xFE9B.name: XBASIC
0xFE9B.comment: Cold-enter Applesoft (or whatever BASIC is in ROM)

0xFF44.name: RTS_FF44
0xFF44.comment: Bare RTS (useful as no-op vector target)

0xFF4A.name: SAVE
0xFF4A.comment: Save A,X,Y,P,S into Monitor's save area ($45-$49)

0xFF58.name: IORTS
0xFF58.comment: Bare RTS used by slot firmware to test addressing (lookup by computing return address - see slot firmware identification convention)

0xFF65.name: MONZ
0xFF65.comment: Monitor entry (clear stack, print '*', GETLN, parse)

0xFFA7.name: GETNUM
0xFFA7.comment: Parse a hex number from input buffer

0xFFBE.name: TOSUB
0xFFBE.comment: Dispatch Monitor command via subroutine table

0xFFCC.name: CHRSRC
0xFFCC.comment: Get next char from input buffer

0xFFD0.name: GETCHR
0xFFD0.comment: Get current char from input buffer

0xFFEF.name: CTRLY_VECTOR
0xFFEF.comment: Monitor's CTRL-Y handler (JMP target chained from $03F8)

0x0000.name: ZERO_PAGE
0x0000.comment: Zero page ($0000-$00FF) - Monitor + Applesoft + DOS allocations

0x0300.name: USER_VECTORS
0x0300.comment: User work area / DOS vectors / Monitor vectors ($0300-$03EF)

0x0400.name: TEXT_PAGE_1
0x0400.comment: Text/lo-res display page 1 ($0400-$07FF). Holes contain screen-hole bytes used by slot firmware

0x0800.name: TEXT_PAGE_2
0x0800.comment: Text/lo-res display page 2 ($0800-$0BFF) - or program area when not using PAGE 2

0x0C00.name: FREE_RAM
0x0C00.comment: Free RAM ($0C00 onwards) until DOS / hi-res / HIMEM

0x6000.name: FREE_OR_DOS_RAM
0x6000.comment: Free RAM or DOS area ($6000 onwards). DOS 3.3 loads upper portion of 48K RAM

0xC000.name: IO_PAGE
0xC000.comment: I/O soft switches and slot device-select page

0xC100.name: SLOT_ROM_AREA
0xC100.comment: Slot firmware ROMs ($C100-$C7FF, 256 bytes per slot)

0xD000.name: BASIC_ROM_OR_LC
0xD000.comment: Applesoft BASIC ROM or language card RAM bank 1/2

0xE000.name: BASIC_ROM_2
0xE000.comment: Applesoft BASIC ROM (continued) or language card RAM

0xA0_const.name: INVERSE_MASK
0xA0_const.comment: INVFLG value $3F masks chars to inverse video range $00-$3F

0x7F_const.name: FLASH_MASK
0x7F_const.comment: INVFLG value $7F masks chars to flashing range $40-$7F

0xFF_const.name: NORMAL_MASK
0xFF_const.comment: INVFLG value $FF passes chars unchanged (normal video)

0xA5_const.name: POWER_UP_XOR
0xA5_const.comment: Autostart cold-boot detection: PWREDUP ($03F4) must equal SOFTEV-high XOR $A5

0x06_const.name: ROMID_IIE
0x06_const.comment: $FBB3 = $06 indicates Apple IIe family (vs $38 II, $EA II+)
